questions.question_text: In Iowa, when does a purchase agreement become binding on all parties?
questions.correct_answer: On acceptance and communication to buyer
distractors: When the buyer signs the initial offer
When the earnest money is deposited
When the seller verbally agrees to terms
questions.explanation: In Iowa, a purchase agreement becomes binding when the seller accepts the offer AND that acceptance is communicated back to the buyer or buyer's agent. Simply signing the acceptance without communication is insufficient to create a binding contract.

questions.question_text: A buyer submits an offer on a home. The seller signs it Saturday evening but the listing agent does not notify the buyer's agent until Monday. When is the contract formed?
distractors: Saturday when the seller signed
Sunday at midnight automatically
When the earnest money check clears
questions.explanation: Iowa requires both acceptance and communication of that acceptance for a binding contract. The seller's signature alone on Saturday did not form the contract. The contract was formed Monday when the buyer's agent received notice of acceptance.

questions.question_text: Which organization publishes the standardized purchase agreement forms most commonly used by Iowa real estate licensees?
distractors: Iowa Real Estate Commission
Iowa Department of Revenue
Iowa Department of Commerce
questions.explanation: The Iowa Association of REALTORS (IAR) publishes the standardized purchase agreement forms most widely used by licensees. The Real Estate Commission does not mandate particular forms. (The Iowa State Bar Association separately publishes official forms — such as deeds and installment contracts — that must be prepared by attorneys under Iowa Court Rule 37.5.)

questions.question_text: A buyer and seller agree on a home sale in a phone call but never sign anything. Under Iowa's Statute of Frauds, is this agreement enforceable?
questions.correct_answer: No, real estate contracts must be in writing
distractors: Yes, text messages are valid contracts
Only if both parties are licensed agents
Yes, if a witness can confirm the texts
questions.explanation: Iowa Code section 622.32(3) bars evidence of a contract for the transfer of an interest in land unless it is in writing and signed by the party charged. A purely oral agreement is generally unenforceable, subject to narrow exceptions such as part performance (Iowa Code 622.33). Note that signed electronic records, such as emails, can satisfy the writing requirement.

questions.question_text: Iowa standardized real estate forms are published by IAR. What is the Commission's role regarding these forms?
questions.correct_answer: The Commission does not mandate their use
distractors: The Commission requires their use statewide
The Commission drafts all approved forms
The Commission must approve every revision
questions.explanation: Unlike states such as Texas where the real estate commission mandates use of specific forms, Iowa's Real Estate Commission does not require licensees to use IAR forms. However, IAR forms are widely used because they are designed to comply with Iowa law.

questions.question_text: Is earnest money legally required to form a valid purchase agreement in Iowa?
distractors: Yes, at least 1% of the sale price
Yes, a minimum deposit of $500
Yes, both parties must contribute
questions.explanation: In Iowa, earnest money is customary in real estate transactions but is not legally required to create a valid contract. Consideration can take other forms. However, most sellers expect earnest money as a sign of good faith.

questions.question_text: A buyer's purchase agreement includes a financing contingency. The lender denies the loan before closing. What is the buyer's position?
questions.correct_answer: Buyer may terminate and recover earnest money
distractors: Buyer must proceed to close without financing
Seller keeps all of the earnest money deposited
Buyer must find a replacement lender immediately
questions.explanation: A financing contingency protects the buyer if they cannot obtain the specified financing. If the lender denies the loan and the contingency has not expired, the buyer can terminate the contract and receive a refund of earnest money.

questions.question_text: An Iowa purchase agreement states closing will occur on June 15, with a 'time is of the essence' clause. The buyer requests a June 20 closing. What must happen?
questions.correct_answer: Both parties must agree in writing to extend
distractors: The 5-day delay is automatically allowed
The seller must accept if buyer has financing
The listing agent can approve the extension
questions.explanation: When 'time is of the essence' is included in an Iowa purchase agreement, all deadlines are strictly enforced. Any extension of the closing date requires a written agreement signed by both parties. Missing the deadline without an extension could constitute a breach.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a common contingency found in Iowa purchase agreements?
questions.correct_answer: Sale of the buyer's current home
distractors: Seller's approval of buyer's employer
Buyer's astrological reading
Approval by the county assessor
questions.explanation: Sale-of-home contingencies are common in Iowa, allowing buyers to make their purchase contingent on selling their current property. Other standard contingencies include financing, inspection, and appraisal contingencies.

questions.question_text: A buyer wants to extend the inspection deadline in an Iowa purchase agreement. How must this be handled?
questions.correct_answer: Written amendment signed by both parties
distractors: The buyer can extend it unilaterally
A verbal agreement between the parties
The home inspector decides the new date
questions.explanation: All modifications to an Iowa purchase agreement, including extending contingency deadlines, must be in writing and signed by both parties. Verbal agreements to modify real estate contracts are not enforceable under the Statute of Frauds.

questions.question_text: An Iowa licensee is asked to draft a complex installment contract with unusual terms. What should the licensee do?
questions.correct_answer: Refer the client to an attorney
distractors: Draft the contract using IAR forms
Create a custom contract from scratch
Ask the broker to draft the document
questions.explanation: Under Iowa Court Rule 37.5, licensees may select, prepare, and complete purchase agreement forms (with written notice to the parties), but they may never prepare deeds or real estate installment sales contracts — those documents must be drafted by an attorney. Preparing an installment contract, complex or simple, would be the unauthorized practice of law.

questions.question_text: What is the scope of activity permitted for Iowa licensees when preparing purchase agreements?
questions.correct_answer: Filling in blanks on approved forms only
distractors: Drafting any contract the client needs
Writing custom addenda with legal clauses
Modifying pre-printed legal language
questions.explanation: Under Iowa Court Rule 37.5, licensees may select, prepare, and complete standard form purchase agreements (plus groundwater hazard statements and declarations of value), provided the parties receive written notice that these are binding legal documents and that competent legal advice should be sought. Licensees cannot draft custom legal documents, prepare deeds or installment contracts, or modify pre-printed legal language — that is the unauthorized practice of law.

questions.question_text: Under Iowa's 2025 real estate commission rules on wholesaling (193E Chapter 22), what requirement applies to residential wholesale transactions?
distractors: Wholesaling is completely prohibited statewide
No license is needed for wholesaling activity
Only licensed brokers may engage in wholesaling
questions.explanation: Implementing Iowa Code section 543B.6A (HF 2394, 2024), the commission's 2025 rules (193E Chapter 22) require wholesalers to give written disclosure to all parties before executing a purchase contract or conveying an equitable interest — identifying all parties and explaining that the wholesaler holds only an equitable interest and may not be able to convey title. Wholesalers must also be licensed brokers or represented by one; violations carry a civil penalty of up to $10,000 or 10% of the sale price, and the buyer or seller may cancel before closing.

questions.question_text: A seller asks their agent to add a hold-harmless clause to the purchase agreement. The agent should:
questions.correct_answer: Advise the seller to consult an attorney
distractors: Add the clause using standard language
Write the clause based on online templates
Have the broker draft the clause
questions.explanation: Drafting a custom hold-harmless clause goes beyond completing standard form documents, which is all Iowa Court Rule 37.5 permits licensees to do. Writing custom legal provisions is the unauthorized practice of law, so the licensee should refer the seller to an attorney.

questions.question_text: Which statement best describes Iowa's title and closing practice?
questions.correct_answer: Attorneys examine abstracts and issue title opinions
distractors: Private title insurance is the standard evidence of title
An attorney must personally conduct every closing
Closings may be performed only by notaries
questions.explanation: Iowa is an abstract state: title is evidenced by an abstract of title examined by an Iowa attorney who issues a title opinion, and Iowa Title Guaranty coverage is built on that opinion — Iowa bans the in-state sale of private title insurance. No law requires an attorney to conduct the closing itself; brokers and independent closers commonly handle closings, though nonlawyer document preparation is limited by Iowa Court Rule 37.5.
